pr.title: Match 16 functions across ft, lb, gr modules
pr.author: itsgrimetime

summary: Attempted broad decomp-matching batch across fighter, library, and ground/stage modules. The PR body reported 16 matched functions, 15 at 100% and one at 98.1%, with configure.py flips from NonMatching to Matching for lbaudio_ax.c, ft_0D31.c, ftCo_Attack100.c, ftKb_Init.c, ftPp_SpecialS.c, ftCh_Init.c, and grzebes.c. The PR was closed unmerged almost immediately because the author reported cascading conflicts from cherry-picked commits and planned a cleaner follow-up.
what_changed: configure.py marked several objects as Matching: melee/lb/lbaudio_ax.c, melee/ft/ft_0D31.c, melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Attack100.c, melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftKb_Init.c, melee/ft/chara/ftPopo/ftPp_SpecialS.c, melee/ft/chara/ftCrazyHand/ftCh_Init.c, and melee/gr/grzebes.c.
Implemented small fighter functions including ftCo_800D7268, ftKb_SpecialN_800F11AC, fn_800F1CA0, fn_800D55B4, fn_800D5A30, fn_80157080, ftCh_Damage2_Phys, and ftPp_SpecialHi_80121DA0.

Added or refined typed declarations in headers, replacing multiple UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS prototypes with concrete signatures such as M2C_UNK ftCo_800D7268(void*), void fn_800263B4(void*), bool grCastle_801CDF54(Vec3*), and s32 fn_801CADBC(void).

Expanded ground/stage code in grcastle, grgarden, grgreatbay, grrcruise, grvenom, grzakogenerator, and grzebes with simple animation setup, callback setup, stage-id checks, cleanup calls, and helper wrappers.
Updated struct knowledge for grcastle by replacing a large padding blob with known fields including bitfields x11_b5_b7/x11_b0_b4 and s32 x14 used by grAnime_801C8138.
Added library matches around lbAudioAx object freeing, lbBgFlash color overlay dispatch through lb_800144C8, and lbRefract display wrappers that reset GX indirect TEV state and invalidate HSD state.
Deleted orig/GALE01/sys/.gitkeep, although the PR context gives no stated reason for that file removal.
The PR was closed before merge because the author cited cascading conflicts between cherry-picked commits.
smart_moves: Converted only the objects claimed to match in configure.py, making build-system matching status explicit and searchable.

Used minimal typed wrappers for many small functions instead of over-expanding unknown systems, e.g. fn_801CADBC simply forwards to grZakoGenerator_801CA8B4(0), and fn_800263B4 null-checks before HSD_ObjFree.
Improved header prototypes alongside implementations, which reduces future UNK_PARAMS spread and helps later callsites type-check.
Introduced narrow struct/bitfield knowledge where needed for matching, especially unkCastle fields at offsets 0x11 and 0x14 rather than leaving the whole region padded.
Reused existing engine helpers such as Fighter_ChangeMotionState, ftAnim_8006EBA4, grAnime_801C8138, Ground_801C2FE0, mpJointSetCb1, and HSD_JObj flag helpers instead of duplicating behavior.

Preserved matching-sensitive code shapes where evidence suggests they were needed, including a volatile temporary assignment in ft_80089824 and pointer-offset field access in functions that still lacked full struct typing.
decomp_lessons: Broad cherry-picked matching batches are risky: this PR was closed unmerged due to cascading conflicts, so future work should split unrelated ft/lb/gr matches into smaller, cleaner PRs or rebase before review.
When matching small functions, update both the C implementation and the header prototype in the same change; this PR repeatedly replaced UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with concrete signatures.
Use partial struct recovery when a function needs only a few fields; grcastle.h shows a useful pattern of naming known offsets while retaining padding for unknown regions.

Stage code often matches through existing ground animation and cleanup helpers plus small stage-id gates; avoid inventing complex logic when the function is just setting flags/callbacks around grAnime_801C8138 or Ground_801C2FE0.

For graphics library wrappers, matching may require restoring render state after delegated display calls, as shown by lbRefract calling hsdDObj.disp and then resetting GX indirect stages and invalidating HSD state.
If a match still relies on M2C_FIELD, byte-offset casts, or M2C_UNK, keep that uncertainty visible rather than pretending the full struct/type is understood.
naming_conventions: Unknown functions kept the project convention fn_ADDRESS, e.g. fn_800F1CA0, fn_800D55B4, fn_80157080, fn_801CADBC, and fn_802040B4.

Module-prefixed symbols followed existing subsystem names: ftCo_ for common fighter, ftKb_ for Kirby, ftPp_ for Popo, ftCh_ for Crazy Hand, grCastle_/grRCruise_/grZebes_ for stages, and lbBgFlash_/lbRefract_/lbAudioAx_ for library modules.
Global data names remained address-based when semantic names were not recovered, e.g. lbl_804D63E0, lbl_804D63DC, ftCh_Init_804DA070, ftPp_Init_804D9860, and grZe_804DB0B0.
Partial struct fields used offset-derived names such as x11_b5_b7, x14, x10C, and x134, consistent with decomp practice when semantic names are not yet known.
Header comments preserved address annotations like /* 0D7268 */, /* 1CDF54 */, and /* 0263B4 */ next to typed prototypes.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used M2C_FIELD(fp, u32*, 0x2238) in fn_800F1CA0 to read an unresolved Fighter field while still implementing the switch/boolean behavior.
Used an anonymous local struct cast in ftCo_800D7268 to model a callback argument containing Fighter_GObj* x0 and int x4.
Used byte-offset casts for not-yet-modeled fields, such as Crazy Hand offsets 0x234C/0x2358 and ground fields around 0xC4/0xC8/0xD4.
Used a volatile s32 temporary in ft_80089824, likely to force a store/load pattern needed for matching.
Recovered motion-state calls by declaring extern float constants and passing them to Fighter_ChangeMotionState for Crazy Hand and Popo functions.
Matched stage helpers through concise state-flag writes, callback assignment, and calls to grAnime_801C8138, mpJointSetCb1, HSD_JObjSetFlagsAll, HSD_JObjClearFlagsAll, and Ground_801C2FE0.
Matched lbRefract wrappers by delegating to hsdDObj.disp and then explicitly resetting GXSetTevDirect(0), GXSetNumIndStages(0), and HSD_StateInvalidate(-1).
review_feedback: decomp-dev bot reported for GALE01: 'No changes'.
No human review comments were present in the slice.
The author closed the PR with the explanation: 'Closing this PR due to cascading conflicts between cherry-picked commits. gonna follow up with a cleaner PR'.
searchable_terms: Match 16 functions across ft lb gr modules
follow_up_queries: Find the cleaner follow-up PR after pr-2015 that split or rebased these matches.
Search for later merged implementations of ftCo_800D7268, ftKb_SpecialN_800F11AC, ftCh_Damage2_Phys, and grZebes_801DCCC8.
Check whether the 98.1% ftPp_SpecialHi_801218AC/801218F8 match was later improved to 100%.
Review later grcastle.h changes to see whether unkCastle x11 bitfields and x14 were renamed semantically.
Audit whether orig/GALE01/sys/.gitkeep deletion was intentional or an artifact of the conflicted batch.
